test_cases.test_case: Submit with Username blank (text required field representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required (violates 'Username and Password are required'); the form does not submit and the user remains on the Login page; the Password field is cleared; no redirect to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Password blank (password required field representative)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required (violates 'Username and Password are required'); the form does not submit and the user remains on the Login page; the Password field is cleared (remains empty); the Username field retains the entered value

test_cases.test_case: Submit with both required fields empty (all-required-fields empty test)

steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Username and Password fields each display inline validation errors indicating they are required (violates 'Username and Password are required'); the form does not submit and the user remains on the Login page; Password field is cleared; no redirect to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Attempt login with non-empty but invalid credentials (credentials constraint violation)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <validly formed username> in the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password for that username> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked due to invalid credentials (violates 'credentials must be valid'); an inline error message appears on the login form indicating the credentials are invalid; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value; no redirect to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Lost password?' link when feature is disabled (precondition not met)
preconditions: Test site with password recovery feature disabled
steps: 1. Click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: 'Lost password?' link is disabled/unresponsive due to feature being disabled (precondition: feature disabled on this test site); clicking it does not navigate to a password recovery page and no recovery dialog/page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to activate disabled 'Lost password?' link
preconditions: 'Lost password?' link is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Locate the 'Lost password?' link on the Login page
2. Click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: Click is blocked; no navigation or password-recovery UI appears and the link remains visually disabled (no modal or page load occurs).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click 'Log in' with validly entered but incorrect credentials
steps: 1. Enter a non-empty Username that does not match any account in the Username field
2. Enter a non-empty Password in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
4. Immediately click the 'Log in' button again
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked; an inline error message is shown indicating authentication failure; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value; only one inline error message instance is visible.

test_cases.test_case: Enter credentials then choose 'Access as a guest'
steps: 1. Enter any value in the Username field
2. Enter any value in the Password field
3. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: 'Access as a guest' succeeds; unauthenticated browsing is granted and the user is not redirected to the Dashboard (no authentication attempt completes using the entered credentials).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid repeated clicks on 'Cookies notice' toggle

2. Immediately click the 'Cookies notice' button again
3. Click the 'Cookies notice' button a third time
test_cases.expected_result: Opening the cookie information succeeds on first click; repeated clicks do not create duplicate overlays or multiple stacked panels — only one cookie usage panel/modal is visible and the UI remains responsive.

3. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search control)

test_cases.test_case: Prev_Month across year boundary decrements the displayed year (January -> December previous year)

steps: 1. Click the Prev_Month button once
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Calendar month and year heading updates to December and the displayed year is the previous year (year decremented by 1); the Calendar Block grid refreshes to show the correct dates for December of the previous year

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive Next_Month clicks result in the final month rendered correctly without stale UI
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page and Calendar Block is visible
Note the current month and year in the calendar heading before starting
steps: 1. Click the Next_Month button
2. Immediately click the Next_Month button again
3. Immediately click the Next_Month button a third time
4. Wait for the calendar UI to finish updating
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Calendar heading displays the month that is three months ahead of the original heading (month and year adjusted accordingly); the calendar grid fully refreshes to that final month, no persistent loading indicator or error message is shown, and no intermediate/stale month remains visible

test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' control is not visible on the page and cannot be clicked; Add_Block_Page cannot be opened from the header while Edit Mode is OFF; no layout change occurs

test_cases.test_case: Move handle (drag icon) is not visible or usable when Edit Mode is off

2. Locate an existing block in the Blocks_Layout
3. Inspect the block for a move handle / move icon
4. Attempt to initiate a drag-and-drop or click the move handle
test_cases.expected_result: The Move handle (Move icon) is not visible on the block while Edit Mode is OFF; the move action cannot be initiated and the layout is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Options menu (Configure/Move/Delete) is not visible or usable when Edit Mode is off
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page with at least one block present
steps: 1. Ensure Edit Mode toggle is OFF
2. Locate the three-dot Options_Menu for an existing block
3. Attempt to open the Options_Menu or activate Configure / Move / Delete actions
test_cases.expected_result: The Options_Menu and its item actions (Configure, Move, Delete) are not visible or accessible while Edit Mode is OFF; none of the configure/move/delete actions can be triggered and the block/layout remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Add a block is blocked when Block_Type dropdown is left blank (required field)
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
steps: 1. Ensure Edit Mode toggle is ON
2. Click the '+ Add a block' button to open Add_Block_Page
3. Do not select any option from the Block_Type dropdown (leave Block_Type blank)
4. Attempt to submit the Add_Block_Page (e.g., press Enter or click the dialog's submit control)
test_cases.expected_result: The Block_Type field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the Add_Block_Page remains open; no block is added to the user's layout and no layout persistence/changes occur

test_cases.test_case: Toggle Edit Mode off while Add a block page is open
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Dashboard initially displays the current saved layout
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode
2. Click the "+ Add a block" button
3. Verify the Add a block page (Block_Type dropdown and Cancel link) is visible
4. Click the Edit Mode toggle to disable Edit Mode
test_cases.expected_result: Add a block page closes and all Edit Mode controls (Reset page to default, + Add a block, move icons, options menus) are hidden; no new block is added to the layout (operation succeeds)

test_cases.test_case: Select a Block_Type and immediately click Reset page to default
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode
2. Click the "+ Add a block" button
3. In the Add a block page, select any option from the Block_Type dropdown
4. Immediately click the Reset page to default button (without waiting for any additional confirmations)
5. Observe the Dashboard layout after Reset completes
test_cases.expected_result: Reset page to default reverts the user's layout to the default and closes the Add a block page; the newly selected block does not appear in the layout after Reset completes (Reset succeeds; no extra block remains)

test_cases.test_case: Add a block then immediately delete it via the block's Options menu to test persistence race
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode
2. Click the "+ Add a block" button
3. In the Add a block page, select any option from the Block_Type dropdown
4. Wait for the Add a block page to close (block appears in layout)
5. Click the Options menu for the newly added block
6. Click the Delete action in the Options menu
7. Click the Edit Mode toggle to disable Edit Mode
8. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode again to verify persisted layout
test_cases.expected_result: Delete action persists and the block is removed from the user's layout; after toggling Edit Mode off and on the block remains absent (delete operation succeeds; no duplicate or ghost block appears)

test_cases.test_case: Remove all blocks using Options → Delete, then verify empty layout persists
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Dashboard initially has one or more blocks present
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode
2. Click the Options menu for the first visible block
3. Click Delete in the Options menu for that block
4. Repeat steps 2–3 for each remaining block until no blocks remain
5. Click the Edit Mode toggle to disable Edit Mode
6. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit Mode
test_cases.expected_result: All Delete actions persist and the dashboard layout is left with no blocks for this user; after toggling Edit Mode off and on the dashboard still shows no blocks (removing all blocks succeeds)

2. Observe the top-right area where the 'Collapse all' link is expected
3. Attempt to click the 'Collapse all' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Collapse all' control is not actionable: either the 'Collapse all' link is not rendered or it is displayed in a disabled state; clicking it does not change the page (no collapse action occurs) and no section collapse animation or state change is performed.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive toggles of a single section
preconditions: There is at least one section displayed and it is currently expanded
That section contains at least one activity
steps: 1. Click the Section_Header (Toggle Section Collapse/Expand) for the target section
2. Click the same Section_Header a second time within 200ms of the first click
3. Click the same Section_Header a third time within 200ms of the previous click
4. Click the same Section_Header a fourth time within 200ms of the previous click
5. Click the same Section_Header a fifth time within 200ms of the previous click
test_cases.expected_result: After the sequence of rapid clicks the final visible Section_Collapse_State reflects the last click (if last click was collapse → section shows collapsed chevron and activities hidden; if last click was expand → section shows expand chevron and activities visible). Toggle action sequence succeeds and no UI error message or visual state inconsistency (e.g., duplicated activity rows or flashing stale content) is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Idempotent behavior when clicking 'Collapse all' twice in a row
preconditions: There are multiple sections with a mixture of expanded and collapsed states
steps: 1. Click the Collapse_All_Link (Collapse all)
2. Immediately click the Collapse_All_Link (Collapse all) again within 1 second
test_cases.expected_result: First click collapses all sections (each section shows collapsed chevron and activities hidden). Second click is idempotent and succeeds: all sections remain collapsed, no error or visual flash is shown, and the UI does not create duplicate section entries or unexpected expansion.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open an activity while its section is collapsed
preconditions: There is at least one section that contains at least one activity
steps: 1. Verify the target section is collapsed (activities are not visible)
2. Attempt to click the Activity_Name link for an activity inside that collapsed section
test_cases.expected_result: Click attempt on Activity_Name is blocked because the activity is not visible/interactive while the section is collapsed; no navigation occurs and no activity detail page is opened. The UI shows the section remains collapsed (collapsed chevron) and no error dialog is shown. If the UI instead auto-expands on attempted click, the auto-expand action succeeds and the subsequent click navigates exactly once to the activity.

test_cases.test_case: Double-click / rapid repeated click on an activity link
preconditions: Target section is expanded and the activity's Activity_Name link is visible and enabled
steps: 1. Click the Activity_Name link for the target activity
2. Immediately click the same Activity_Name link a second time before navigation completes (within 200ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Only one navigation to the activity/resource occurs; the second rapid click does not cause a duplicate navigation or duplicate resource loads. The navigation action succeeds and the Course Page does not show duplicate entries or error messages resulting from the rapid repeated clicks.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Message button does not create duplicate message composers
preconditions: Signed-in user who can view the teacher's Profile page
steps: 1. Sign in as the user
2. Navigate to the teacher's Profile page
3. Click the Message button
4. Immediately click the Message button again (second click within ~1 second)
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single messaging composer is visible after the rapid double-click; the second click focuses the existing composer and does not create a duplicate composer window or multiple composer instances (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Login activity exact date and relative-time indicator for today, yesterday, and a future date
preconditions: Three teacher users exist with Last access values set to: (a) today's date/time, (b) yesterday's date/time, and (c) a future date/time
Tester can navigate to each teacher's Profile page
steps: 1. Sign in as any user with permission to view profiles
2. Navigate to the Profile page of the teacher whose Last access = today
3. Observe the 'Last access' exact date display and the relative time indicator
4. Navigate to the Profile page of the teacher whose Last access = yesterday
5. Observe the 'Last access' exact date display and the relative time indicator
6. Navigate to the Profile page of the teacher whose Last access = a future date
7. Observe the 'Last access' exact date display and the relative time indicator
test_cases.expected_result: For the 'today' record, the Profile page displays the exact date/time and a relative-time indicator representing the same-day offset (e.g., 'Today' / 'X minutes/hours ago') (succeeds). For the 'yesterday' record, the Profile page displays the exact date/time and a relative-time indicator representing one day ago (succeeds). For the future-date record, the Profile page still displays the exact future date/time and a relative-time indicator that indicates a future offset (e.g., 'in X days') or explicitly marks the timestamp as future/unexpected (succeeds with future-indicator). No UI layout break or omission of the date/time is acceptable.

test_cases.test_case: Expand all collapsible sections via the 'Expand all' link
steps: 1. Click the 'Expand all' link at the top right of the form
test_cases.expected_result: expands all collapsible sections

test_cases.test_case: Save profile with required fields only using 'Update profile'
steps: 1. Enter <first name> in the First name field
2. Enter <last name> in the Last name field
3. Enter <valid email> in the Email address field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: refreshes the profile page

test_cases.test_case: Upload a new picture via drag-and-drop, add interests and description, then save
steps: 1. Expand the 'User picture' section if collapsed
2. Drag-and-drop a <valid image file> onto the Picture upload area
3. Enter <picture description> in the Picture description field
4. Expand the 'Interests' section if collapsed
5. Enter <interest tag> in the Interests tag input and confirm the tag
6. Expand the 'General' section if collapsed
7. Enter <description content> in the Description rich text editor
8. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: refreshes the profile page

test_cases.test_case: Add an Additional name row and save so the new alternative name is visible
steps: 1. Expand the 'Additional names' section if collapsed
2. Click 'Add Row' in the Additional names section
3. Enter <alternative name> in the new Alternative Name field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: New row appears in the Additional names list with Alternative Name = <alternative name>

test_cases.test_case: Cancel editing and exit without saving changes
steps: 1. Make a visible change in any field (for example enter <temporary value> in the City/Town field)
2. Click the 'Cancel' button
test_cases.expected_result: exits without making changes

test_cases.test_case: Leave required text field (First_Name) blank and submit
preconditions: Navigate to the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Clear the First_Name field (leave it blank)
2. Enter <valid last name> into the Last_Name field
3. Enter <valid email address> into the Email_Address field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the First_Name field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Leave required email field (Email_Address) blank and submit
preconditions: Navigate to the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Clear the Email_Address field (leave it blank)
2. Enter <valid first name> into the First_Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> into the Last_Name field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email_Address field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Submit with ALL required fields empty
preconditions: Navigate to the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Clear the First_Name field
2. Clear the Last_Name field
3. Clear the Email_Address field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on First_Name, Last_Name and Email_Address fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; profile is not created or updated

test_cases.test_case: Enter an invalid email format and attempt to save
preconditions: Navigate to the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> into the Email_Address field
2. Enter <valid first name> into the First_Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> into the Last_Name field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email_Address field indicating the value is not a valid email address; the form does not submit; profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Upload a file that violates site upload constraints and submit
preconditions: Navigate to the Edit profile page
steps: 1. Attach <file violating site upload constraints> to the Picture_Upload control (via drag-and-drop or file picker)
2. Enter <valid first name> into the First_Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> into the Last_Name field
4. Enter <valid email address> into the Email_Address field
5. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Upload control displays an error indicating the file violates site upload constraints (e.g. file type or size invalid); the form does not submit; profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to access Edit profile page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Edit profile page URL while signed out
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page (Edit profile page is not accessible); no profile edit controls are shown to the unauthenticated user

test_cases.test_case: Upload file exactly at the site's maximum allowed upload size and save
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
User has access to a file exactly at the site's maximum allowed upload size
steps: 1. Expand the 'User picture' collapsible section
2. Drag-and-drop the file that is exactly the site's maximum allowed upload size onto the Picture_Upload area
3. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Upload and save succeeds; clicking 'Update profile' submits successfully, the profile page refreshes, and the new picture is visible on the profile page (picture replaced by the uploaded file).

test_cases.test_case: Upload file one unit over the site's maximum allowed upload size and attempt to save
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
User has access to a file that is one unit (e.g., 1 byte) larger than the site's maximum allowed upload size
steps: 1. Expand the 'User picture' collapsible section
2. Drag-and-drop the file that is one unit over the site's maximum allowed upload size onto the Picture_Upload area
3. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Upload is blocked / error shown; submission is blocked and an inline error is shown near the Picture_Upload field indicating the file exceeds the site's maximum allowed upload size, and the profile page does not refresh.

test_cases.test_case: Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Description rich text editor and save
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
The Description field is visible in the General section
steps: 1. Expand the 'General' collapsible section
2. Place the cursor in the Description rich text editor
3. Paste a continuous text block of 200+ characters into the Description editor
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission proceeds; after clicking 'Update profile' the profile page refreshes. The saved Description either (a) displays the full long text on the profile detail page (accepted), or (b) displays a visibly truncated Description with an explicit truncation indicator/ellipsis or inline message indicating the Description was trimmed. Either outcome must be visible on the refreshed profile page.

test_cases.test_case: Enter emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters into Description and First Name fields and save
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
General and Description fields are visible
steps: 1. Expand the 'General' collapsible section
2. Enter emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters into the First_Name field
3. Enter emoji and non-Latin Unicode characters into the Description rich text editor
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Unicode input handling visible; after clicking 'Update profile' the profile page refreshes. The saved values either appear exactly as entered (emoji and Unicode characters visible) or an inline validation error is shown indicating unsupported characters. The visible outcome must clearly show whether the characters were saved or rejected.

test_cases.test_case: Enter leading and trailing whitespace in required First Name and Last Name and save
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
First_Name and Last_Name fields are visible in the General section
steps: 1. Expand the 'General' collapsible section
2. Enter a value with leading and trailing spaces into the First_Name field
3. Enter a value with leading and trailing spaces into the Last_Name field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace trimming behavior visible; submission succeeds. On the refreshed profile page, the First and Last name values are shown without leading or trailing spaces (whitespace trimmed). If the system treats whitespace-only as empty, submission is blocked and an inline required-field error is shown for the affected field(s).

test_cases.test_case: Add an Additional Name, remove it, then save (repeating group add-then-remove all)
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
The 'Additional names' collapsible section is visible
steps: 1. Expand the 'Additional names' collapsible section
2. Click to add a new item in Additional_Names_List
3. Enter a value into the Alternative_Name field for the new item
4. Remove the Additional_Names_List item just added
5. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission succeeds; because Additional_Names_List has min=0, the form submits successfully and the refreshed profile page shows no additional names entries (no residual empty/ghost entries).

test_cases.test_case: Immediately press browser Back after successful Update profile to check for duplicate or stale edit form behavior
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
User has made a change that can be saved (e.g., change Description)
steps: 1. Click the 'Update profile' button to save changes
2. Wait for the profile page to refresh (successful save)
3. Immediately press the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate or unintended state; after pressing Back the browser either (a) shows the refreshed profile page (not the edit form), or (b) shows the edit form but with fields blank or reflecting the saved values (not causing a second save). No additional profile entities are created and only the single intended save exists.

test_cases.test_case: Make multiple edits then click Cancel to ensure no changes are saved
preconditions: Edit Profile form is open on the Edit Profile page
steps: 1. Expand the 'General' collapsible section
2. Change the First_Name field
3. Change the Description rich text editor
4. Click the 'Cancel' button
test_cases.expected_result: Cancel exits without saving; the profile page is shown (or user is navigated away) and the previously saved profile values remain unchanged (the changes made in the edit form are not persisted).

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Log out' button terminates session and redirects to Login page
preconditions: The application header with the 'Log out' button is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
test_cases.expected_result: terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page; the Login page is displayed and the login form prompting for credentials is visible

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use Log out control while not authenticated (precondition violation) - button visibility
steps: 1. Open the application's main page while not authenticated
2. Inspect the page header and navigation area for a 'Log out' button
3. If a 'Log out' button is present, attempt to click it
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Log out' button is not visible/available to the anonymous user (control is not present or is disabled); clicking it is not possible. The application does not send a logout request and the user remains unauthenticated (no session was terminated).

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, accessing a protected page is blocked (post-logout access requires re-authentication)
steps: 1. Log in as <role>
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. Observe that the application redirects to the Login page (post-logout)
4. From the browser address bar, navigate to a protected page at <protected page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to <protected page URL> is blocked and the user is redirected to the Login page; protected page content is not displayed and re-authentication is required (session remains terminated).

test_cases.test_case: Direct access to the Logout endpoint while unauthenticated is blocked/redirects to login
steps: 1. Open a browser while not authenticated
2. Enter the application's Logout endpoint URL (<logout endpoint URL>) in the address bar and navigate there
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects the anonymous user to the Login page instead of performing a logout; no session termination occurs and no protected content is exposed.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click the Log out button
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button once
2. Immediately click the 'Log out' button a second time (within 1 second of the first click)
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single logout action succeeds: the user is redirected to the login page and the UI shows the login page. The second click is ignored (no duplicate navigation or duplicate login page stacks are visible).

test_cases.test_case: Log out in one tab, then refresh a protected page in another tab
preconditions: User is authenticated with two browser tabs open on protected pages (Tab A and Tab B)
steps: 1. In Tab A, click the 'Log out' button
2. In Tab B, click the browser refresh button (reload the protected page)
test_cases.expected_result: The logout in Tab A succeeds and redirects Tab A to the login page. In Tab B, the refresh is blocked from showing protected content: Tab B is redirected to the login page (access to protected page is blocked).

test_cases.test_case: Click Log out while the browser is offline
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
Browser network set to offline (no network connectivity)
steps: 1. With network offline, click the 'Log out' button
test_cases.expected_result: The client-side logout flow succeeds: the app clears the local authenticated state and displays the login page (user is shown the login page). Subsequent navigation to a protected page remains blocked (the login page is shown).

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back after successful logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
2. After being redirected to the login page, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Returning via the browser Back button does not restore access to the previously viewed protected page: the user remains on or is redirected back to the login page and access to protected content is blocked.
